prerequisites: A LaunchDarkly account with permission to create an API access token. By default, members can create tokens scoped to their existing permissions, and Admin or Owner members can create broader-scoped tokens.
An API access token generated in LaunchDarkly Organization settings under Authorization, used as the access_token config field.

authMethods.name: API access token
authMethods.summary: Authenticate using a LaunchDarkly API access token supplied via the access_token field. LaunchDarkly authenticates REST API requests by sending the token directly as the value of the Authorization header with no Bearer prefix. Scope the token to the smallest role required, and Reader is sufficient for read-only extraction.

setupSteps.title: Create an API access token in LaunchDarkly
setupSteps.body: Click the gear icon in the left sidebar to open Organization settings, go to Authorization, and in the Access tokens section select Create token. Enter a human-readable name and assign a role (Reader, Writer, Admin, or Owner, or a custom role if your subscription includes them). Give the token the smallest scope required. Reader is enough for read-only data extraction.

setupSteps.title: Copy and store the token immediately
setupSteps.body: The API access token is visible only once, immediately after creation. If you leave or refresh the page, it is obscured and no longer retrievable. Copy it and store it securely before navigating away.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied token as the access_token field in the connector configuration. The connector passes it as the Authorization header value when calling the LaunchDarkly REST API.

sources.title: Creating API access tokens | LaunchDarkly Documentation
